title: Final Closures: Storage Substrate & Profile Parity (Revised)
author: orchestrator
summary: Two remaining closures to reach full definition: (1) Storage Substrate — Postgres becomes the only live write backend, DuckDB is wired as analytical sidecar, all Database.use paths go through the adapter seam, and SQLite is removed; (2) Profile Parity — repo source agents, approvals, permissions match fundamental installed OpenCode agent model (orchestrator, subagents, approvals, permissions). Skill parity is explicitly out of scope (verified from installed set this repo cannot reproduce). Execution is sequential and must not skip evidence gates.

current_state.database_adapter: Fully implemented in packages/opencode/src/storage/adapter.ts with DrizzleLikeClient Interface (query, transaction, afterCommit), SQLiteAdapter impl, PgAdapter impl, and defaultLayer selecting based on OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL presence. Phase 1 adapter hardening complete (13 unit tests passing, afterCommit refactored, discriminated transaction options, retry support). adapter.ts lines 88 and 105 internally use Database.use and Database.transaction by design — SQLiteAdapter wraps the legacy singleton; these are not targets for conversion.
current_state.postgres_schema_files: All 8 .pg.sql.ts files exist: session.pg.sql.ts, project.pg.sql.ts, account.pg.sql.ts, workspace.pg.sql.ts, event.pg.sql.ts, share.pg.sql.ts, schema.pg.sql.ts, data-migration.pg.sql.ts. drizzle.config.pg.ts exists with dialect: postgresql. DrizzleLikeClient supports pg-core tables structurally. All 8 .sqlite.sql.ts files also exist (SQLite-native variants alongside .pg.sql.ts counterparts).
current_state.adapter_consumers: 82 DatabaseAdapter references across codebase. Multiple consumers already use yield* DatabaseAdapter.Service pattern. ONLY 3 raw Database.use calls remain (outside of adapter.ts internal wrappers): workspace.ts:986 (waitEvent sync callback), server/shared/fence.ts:15 (plain load function), server/projectors.ts:15 (sync convertEvent callback). adapter.ts:88,105 intentionally wraps Database.use/transaction by design as SQLiteAdapter. No data-migration.ts or project/project.ts raw calls exist.
current_state.coordination_tool: Exists with wave types: learning, critique, execution, validation, stress, repair, documentation, publish, report. 7 coordination tool source files exist as untracked (checkpoint-tools.ts, checkpoint.ts, coordination.ts, failure-tools.ts, file-lock.ts, read-messages.ts, report.ts, search_replace.ts, send-message.ts, test.ts, validate.ts).
current_state.sqlite_schema_files: All 8 .sqlite.sql.ts files exist alongside .pg.sql.ts variants. Runtime imports still reference .sqlite.sql.ts variants (~35-42 import sites). The 8 original .sql.sql.ts files (no prefix) have been deleted from the working tree.
current_state.sqlite_backend: db.bun.ts and db.node.ts still active. #db import alias points to bun-sqlite. json-migration.ts still active. SQLite PRAGMAs already gated behind !Flag.OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL in db.ts lines 104-111 — PRAGMA gating is already done and removed from plan scope.
current_state.postgres_ddl: migration-pg/ directory does not exist. drizzle-kit generate has not been run against drizzle.config.pg.ts.
current_state.data_migration: No migrate-to-pg CLI command exists. SQLite-to-Postgres one-way migration not implemented.
current_state.duckdb_analytical: Zero DuckDB references anywhere in the codebase (no imports, no config, no schemas, no sidecar).
current_state.ci_postgres: No Postgres service container in GitHub Actions. No test/fixture/pg.ts exists. No CI-backed Postgres test runner.
current_state.coordination_persistence: coordination.ts is purely in-memory Maps. No disk-backed or database-backed persistent coordination state.
current_state.credential_isolation: packages/opencode/src/effect/database-config.ts already exists (as untracked file) with Config.redacted('OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL'). Phase ~40% pre-done. Remaining work: wire into app-runtime.ts and bootstrap-runtime.ts runtime layers, replace remaining process.env reads.
current_state.existing_plan_approval: docs/json/opencode/approvals/plan_approval.v1.json exists with candidate_boundary_identifier=pg_backend_migration.v3.json, plan_revision=3, and 3 review cycles. This approval is for a DIFFERENT plan document (pg_backend_migration) — not for this final_closures_plan. The existing approval does not gate this plan's execution.

profile_parity.orchestrator_agent: No orchestrator.md in .opencode/agent/. No wave-model orchestrator enforcement.
profile_parity.subagents: No critic/reviewer, executor, validator, stress, or repair agent profiles in .opencode/agent/.
profile_parity.approval_gates: plan_approval.v1.json schema exists as doc artifact. red_team_approval and repair_plan_approval schemas do not exist.
profile_parity.permissions: opencode.jsonc has empty permissions — no read/search/task-only enforcement for orchestrator.
profile_parity.installed_skills: Only 2 skills in repo (effect, improve-codebase-architecture). Installed OpenCode has ~13 skills. Skill parity is out of scope — installed set cannot be verified from this repo source.
profile_parity.legacy_profiles: Legacy reviewer profiles and no long-horizon senior-engineer behavior default.

current_state.typecheck_baseline: 280 pre-existing errors (Effect v4 beta Layer R-type mismatches in test files, not caused by storage work).

plan_approval_transparency.note: The existing approval artifact is for the pg_backend_migration plan track, not for this final_closures plan. A new plan_approval.v1.json must be created for this plan before execution.

Expected ranges: ~59-67 modified files (from prior pg_backend_migration work), ~37 untracked files (new tool files, schema files, test files from parallel work).

files_in_scope.strategy: additive edit for DuckDB adapter registration and pg migration wiring. adapter.ts:88,105 are INTENTIONAL SQLiteAdapter internals — do NOT convert.

dirty_file_audit.conflict_policy: If any edit would remove or conflict with existing modifications (from parallel agents), stop and report the conflict. Preserve all pre-existing modifications exactly. Use expected_before_sha256 on all search_replace operations on dirty files. Never discard other lanes' work.

tracks.name: Storage Substrate
tracks.exit_condition: Postgres is the only live write backend for runtime state. DuckDB is wired as the analytical/read-model sidecar. A one-way SQLite-to-Postgres data migration exists. All 3 remaining raw Database.use paths are behind the adapter seam (excluding adapter.ts internal wrappers). SQLite runtime files and SQLite-native schemas are removed. Typecheck and test suite pass against Postgres.

phases.name: Generate Postgres DDL + Migration Directory
phases.description: Run drizzle-kit generate against drizzle.config.pg.ts to produce initial Postgres migration in migration-pg/. Create migration-pg/ directory with the generated SQL files. Apply migration on startup when Postgres backend is active (modify db.pg.ts init to run migrations). Verify generated DDL is valid Postgres (no SQLite-specific syntax like json_extract, no PRAGMA statements).
files_changed: packages/opencode/migration-pg/ (new directory with generated DDL files)
packages/opencode/src/storage/db.pg.ts (add migration loading path: run generated SQL on first connect)
phases.acceptance: drizzle-kit generate runs successfully with zero errors. migration-pg/ directory contains valid Postgres DDL. Migration applies on Postgres backend startup. Syntax verified: no SQLite-specific functions or PRAGMAs in generated DDL.

phases.name: DuckDB Analytical Sidecar
phases.description: Install DuckDB dependencies (@duckdb/duckdb-wasm or duckdb npm package). Create analytical schema definitions using DuckDB-native SQL (parquet-backed reads, JSON extraction). Create db.duckdb.ts init function. Register DuckDB layer in app-runtime.ts and bootstrap-runtime.ts. Wire DuckDB as read-model sidecar — not as write backend. Analytical queries route to DuckDB, transactional writes route to Postgres. Add #db-duckdb import alias to package.json. Add DuckDB adapter class to adapter.ts (read-only Interface subset). Update DrizzleLikeClient to accept DuckDB structurally if needed.
files_changed: packages/opencode/package.json (add @duckdb/duckdb-wasm or duckdb npm package, add #db-duckdb alias)
packages/opencode/bun.lock
packages/opencode/src/storage/db.duckdb.ts (new — DuckDB init function with analytical query support)
packages/opencode/src/storage/adapter.ts (add DuckDBAdapter or read-only layer registration)
packages/opencode/src/effect/app-runtime.ts (add DuckDB layer provision)
packages/opencode/src/effect/bootstrap-runtime.ts (add DuckDB layer provision)
packages/opencode/src/storage/schema.duckdb.ts (new — DuckDB analytical schema definitions if needed)
phases.acceptance: DuckDB dependency installs. db.duckdb.ts creates DuckDB connection. app-runtime.ts and bootstrap-runtime.ts provide DuckDB layer. Analytical queries can route to DuckDB. typecheck passes. DuckDBAdapter compiles. Existing Postgres transaction path unaffected. DuckDB must not shadow Postgres writes — read-model isolation verified.
blocking_defects: Phase 1.0 (DDL generated) complete — schema source of truth established before analytical layer is added

phases.name: Wire Credential Isolation in Runtime Layers
phases.description: database-config.ts already exists (untracked) with Config.redacted('OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL'). Remaining work: wire DatabaseConfig Effect layer into app-runtime.ts and bootstrap-runtime.ts. Replace any remaining process.env OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL reads at module load time. Ensure credentials never leak into subprocess env. Document in database_security_doctrine.v1.json.
files_changed: packages/opencode/src/effect/database-config.ts (already exists — may need refinement for layer wiring)
packages/opencode/src/storage/db.ts (remove remaining process.env reads, accept config via Effect context if not already done)
packages/opencode/src/storage/db.pg.ts (accept config via Effect context instead of process.env)
packages/opencode/src/effect/app-runtime.ts (wire DatabaseConfig layer)
packages/opencode/src/effect/bootstrap-runtime.ts (wire DatabaseConfig layer)
phases.acceptance: No OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL process.env reads at module load time. Credentials are Redacted until explicit unwrap at connection point. typecheck passes. Tests pass with mock credential provider. database-config.ts is properly wired into both runtime layers.
blocking_defects: Phase 1.0 (DDL generated) complete — schemas underpin credential scope

Do NOT touch adapter.ts lines 88,105 — those are intentional SQLiteAdapter internals wrapping the legacy singleton. Do NOT touch data-migration.ts, project/project.ts, or sync/index.ts — evidence confirms zero raw calls remain in those files.

All 3 conversions produce identical results verified by typecheck and tests. typecheck passes. Tests pass with both SQLiteAdapter and PgAdapter.

Add adapter integration tests for PgAdapter running against real Postgres.

CLI command registered in cmd/index.ts.

deferred_seams_after_1.8: DuckDB analytical query integration into agent tools and Ralph scanner — DuckDB is wired but not yet consumed by agent-facing tools for synthesized conclusions.

PGlite full feature verification against all Postgres-specific DDL features (partitioning, row-level security, etc.) — deferred to proof-of-use.

tracks.name: Profile Parity (Fundamental Agent Model)
tracks.exit_condition: Repo source (.opencode/agent/, opencode.jsonc) can reproduce the fundamental installed OpenCode agent behavior: orchestrator agent with wave-model subagents, approval artifact gates, proper permission restrictions, and validator self-audit rules. Skill parity explicitly deferred to separate work (installed skill set cannot be verified from this repo).
tracks.note_on_paths: .opencode/agent/ is the target directory for agent profiles. opencode.jsonc is the target file for permissions. opencode.jsonc has empty permissions — executor must verify exact structure before editing. Some profile parity work targets src/agent/ (source-level agent config) vs .opencode/agent/ (repo-local config). Executor must distinguish between the two and verify exact target path before editing.

phases.name: Orchestrator Agent Profile With Wave Model
phases.description: Create .opencode/agent/orchestrator.md with wave-model orchestration behavior: learning-first, delegate-first, no-self-inspection rule, approval-artifact gates (plan_approval.v1.json, red_team_approval.v1.json, repair_plan_approval.v1.json) as first-class enforced gates. Orchestrator reads current revision count from state JSON and halts if exceeds 5. Orchestrator enforces the chronology state machine (code commit → yield → audit receipt → commit audit). Orchestrator remains read/search/task-only — no mutation capabilities beyond sending messages and reading messages.
files_changed: .opencode/agent/orchestrator.md (new — wave-model orchestrator agent profile with approval gate enforcement)
phases.acceptance: orchestrator.md exists with wave-model instructions matching installed behavior. Approval artifacts referenced by path. Chronology state machine encoded. Orchestrator restricted to read/search/task-only.

phases.name: Wave-Model Subagent Profiles
phases.description: Create subagent profiles for each wave type: critic/reviewer (long-horizon senior engineer — 3 architectural proposals per review), executor (additive edits, dirty-file preservation, blast radius control), validator (self-validation-before-handoff rule, schema validation, bounded checks), stress (hostile pass with 5-minute review cycle, attack concurrency/contention/recovery/leaks/input), repair (JSON repair directives, no deadlock loops).
files_changed: .opencode/agent/critic.md (new — long-horizon senior engineer with 3-proposal requirement per review)
.opencode/agent/executor.md (new — additive edits, blast radius control, dirty-file preservation)
.opencode/agent/validator.md (new — self-validation-before-handoff, schema validation, bounded checks)
.opencode/agent/stress.md (new — hostile pass, 'break my newest mechanism', attack surface enumeration)
.opencode/agent/repair.md (new — JSON repair directives, delta-focused, no deadlock loops)
phases.acceptance: All 5 subagent profiles exist. Critic encodes three-proposal requirement. Validator encodes self-validation-before-handoff. Stress encodes focused hostile pass. Repair encodes JSON directive consumption. Each profile references its approval gate dependencies.
blocking_defects: Phase 2.1 complete — subagents must reference orchestrator gate

phases.name: Permission Restrictions & Role Enforcement
phases.description: Update opencode.jsonc with proper permission restrictions. Orchestrator: read/search/task-only (no mutation). Executor: write allowed but limited to target files. Critic/reviewer: read-only. Validator: read + schema-validate only. Stress: read + override-approved only. Repair: write allowed but limited to repair delta. Enforce AGENTS.md conventions: no git amend/force-push, no git restore/checkout/reset/clean for undo, no dirty file discarding. SPECIAL HANDLING: opencode.jsonc is at .opencode/opencode.jsonc and its permissions are verified by the OpenCode config loader, not by a typecheck. Validate by loading the config in a test, not by typecheck.
files_changed: .opencode/opencode.jsonc (update permissions: read/search/task for orchestrator, write-limited for executor, read-only for reviewer, validate-only for validator, stress-limited, repair-delta for repair)
.opencode/agent/permissions.md (new — permission doctrine documenting role-to-permission mapping)
phases.acceptance: opencode.jsonc has non-empty permissions. Orchestrator denied write. Executor allowed write but limited. Reviewer read-only. Validator read + validate. All roles verify against AGENTS.md conventions. Permissions validated via OpenCode config loader (not typecheck) in a test harness.
blocking_defects: Phase 2.1 complete — orchestrator profile needs matching permissions

phases.description: Run end-to-end validation of all profiles: verify orchestrator delegates to subagents, subagents respect permissions, approval gates enforced, stress pass produces findings, repair consumes directives. Create e2e test profile or validation script. Document any gaps between repo profiles and installed behavior as deferred seams or out-of-scope findings. Note: skill parity (Phase 2.5 in v1) is explicitly out of scope — the installed skill set has ~13 skills vs 2 in repo, and this cannot be reliably verified from source. The fundamental agent model (orchestrator, subagents, approvals, permissions) is the scope.

Phase 2.3 (approval gates) complete
Phase 2.4 (permissions) complete

repair_limit_rationale: Increased from 10 to 20 total, per-phase from 2 to 4, per reviewer finding that v1 limits were too tight for 16 effective phases (including sub-phases 1.6a/1.6b/1.7a/1.7b/1.7c).

rollback_strategy.method: Per-phase checkpoint commits with pinned SQLite version retained through Phase 1.6b
rollback_strategy.pre_1.6a_code_rollback: git revert the most recent checkpoint commit. Pre-1.6a work is purely additive (new files, adapter additions, schema additions) and does not remove SQLite. DuckDB and credential isolation changes are backward-compatible.
rollback_strategy.post_1.6b_data_rollback: Unset OPENCODE_DATABASE_URL. Rename opencode.db.pre-pg-migration back to opencode.db. Postgres drops are reversible by reapplying the initial migration-pg/ DDL. Migration flag file removed.
rollback_strategy.post_1.7a_rollback: git revert the Phase 1.7a checkpoint commit + restore .db.pre-pg-migration. Phase 1.7a is the first destructive removal phase (removes db.bun.ts, db.node.ts, json-migration.ts). Pre-1.7a rollback path available as long as Phase 1.7a is not committed.
rollback_strategy.pre_1.7a_stoppage: Phase 1.6b (data migration verification) and Phase 1.7a (runtime file removal) are gated by explicit approval. Do not proceed to Phase 1.7a until Phase 1.6b migration is verified in CI and all adapter consumer conversion is complete.
rollback_strategy.track_2_rollback: Track 2 (Profile Parity) is entirely additive (new files in .opencode/agent/, .opencode/skills/). Rollback is git revert of the checkpoint commit. No permanent destructive changes.

data_integrity_checks: Pre-migration (Phase 1.6a): FK constraint audit on existing SQLite data. Repair violations before migration begins.
Post-migration (Phase 1.6b): Row count per table + SHA256 checksum match between SQLite source and Postgres destination.
Rollback (Phase 1.6b): Verify no data loss after rollback by comparing pre-copy checksums.
Phase 1.3: Verify workspace.ts waitEvent, projectors.ts convertEvent, fence.ts load produce identical results with DatabaseAdapter.Service as with raw Database.use.
Phase 1.1 (DuckDB): DuckDB must not shadow Postgres writes. Verify read-model isolation — DuckDB queries do not write through to Postgres.
Phase 1.7c: grep for SQLite patterns (sqliteTable, bun-sqlite, better-sqlite3, db.bun, node-sqlite3, .sqlite.sql.ts) in src/ before and after cleanup.
Phase 2.4: opencode.jsonc permissions validated by OpenCode config loader, not typecheck. Verify in test harness.
evidence_artifacts_produced: docs/json/opencode/approvals/plan_approval.v1.json — plan approval artifact (new, for this plan)
out_of_scope: Skill parity (v1 Phase 2.5) — installed skill set has ~13 skills; repo has 2. Installed set cannot be reliably verified from this repo source. Deferred to separate skill-parity workstream.

DuckDB full analytical query optimization and integration into agent-facing tools (DuckDB is wired but agent tools do not yet consume DuckDB for synthesized conclusions — documented in deferred seams)
effect-drizzle-pg bridge package (DrizzleLikeClient wrapper pattern is intentional boundary)
New database-backed features beyond migration and sidecar wiring
PGlite full feature verification against all Postgres DDL features (deferred to proof-of-use)
DuckDB Wasm in-browser support (desktop-native parquet reads are sufficient)
Migration of existing CI workflows beyond Postgres service container addition
Architectural changes to Ralph scanner to consume DuckDB analytical results

full_definition_exit_conditions.postgres_is_only_write_backend: Phase 1.7c (import updates) and Phase 1.8 (verification) complete. No SQLite runtime files remain. #db alias points to db.pg.ts. All 3 remaining raw Database.use calls migrated to adapter. adapter.ts:88,105 INTENTIONALLY excluded.
full_definition_exit_conditions.duckdb_wired_as_analytical_sidecar: Phase 1.1 (DuckDB) complete. db.duckdb.ts exists. app-runtime.ts and bootstrap-runtime.ts provide DuckDB layer. Analytical queries can route to DuckDB.
full_definition_exit_conditions.sqlite_to_postgres_migration_story: Phase 1.6b (data migration) complete. migrate-to-pg CLI command exists and is documented with rollback and checksums.
full_definition_exit_conditions.all_database_use_behind_adapter_seam: Phase 1.3 (adapter consumers) complete. Zero raw Database.use calls outside of storage/adapter.ts internal wrappers.
full_definition_exit_conditions.agent_tools_return_decisions_receipts: Phase 2.1-2.5 (agent profiles) complete. Orchestrator agent enforces artifact-based handoff with approvals and receipts.
full_definition_exit_conditions.repo_matches_installed_behavior_fundamen: Phase 2.1-2.5 (Profile Parity track) complete. Orchestrator, subagents, approvals, permissions match installed OpenCode fundamental model. Skill parity explicitly out of scope.
full_definition_exit_conditions.critics_as_long_horizon_senior_engineers: Phase 2.2 (critic profile) complete. Critic encodes three-proposal requirement per review.
full_definition_exit_conditions.orchestrator_read_search_task_only: Phase 2.4 (permissions) complete. opencode.jsonc enforces orchestrator role restrictions with proper OpenCode config loader validation.
full_definition_exit_conditions.approval_and_review_artifacts_real_versi: Phase 2.3 (approval schemas) complete. All 3 approval schemas exist with JSON Schema definitions and versioned artifacts.
full_definition_exit_conditions.sqlite_and_legacy_profiles_removed: Phase 1.7c (import updates) and Phase 2.1-2.5 (profile cleanup) complete. No SQLite in src/. No legacy profiles — all profiles match installed wave-model structure.
full_definition_exit_conditions.proven_under_real_boundaries: Phase 1.8 (typecheck recovery) and Phase 2.5 (e2e validation) complete. Validation, typecheck, stress, and repair cycles all verified.
